title: End of Evangelion OST art what is it
display_title: End of Evangelion OST art what is it
wikitext:
[]: End of Evangelion OST art - what is it?             Quote Post by lucyac » Wed Jan 07, 2026 11:50 am (Sorry if this isn't the right place for this post, feel free to move accordingly) I'm the kind of person who likes collecting media on my own drives, music included as it's my biggest interest. Part of this is having all the album art in good quality so I can look at all the funny little pictures whenever I'm listening to stuff. Herein lies something that has jumped to the front of my mind time and time again - what am I looking at? The brilliant artwork for the EoE soundtrack always draws my eyes with the brilliant dazzling red and orange colour palette. Looking at it, to me it resembles the reflection of fire in water with something dark and sinister looming at the bottom of the frame. What makes it more interesting is the central spillage of light sort of resembles a face as well, but a very distorted one. Perhaps intentional or unintentional paraedolia? Something else that I love is the translucent red covering on the CD case that makes it look even more saturated. Whatever I'm looking at, it fits the movie and soundtrack very well. Let me know what you think!

[]: Re: End of Evangelion OST art - what is it?             Quote Post by Diego the Lego » Mon Jan 12, 2026 1:03 am If I had to say I think it's an artistic representation of Human Instrumentality with the all souls combined into one collective. It would make sense since in the film during the the third impact everyone turns into orange LCL similar to the palette of the artwork on the OST, but that's just my opinion.
